assumptions: The intragroup dividend receivable gives rise to a recognised foreign-currency-denominated position or a forecast intragroup foreign-currency cash flow considered in the consolidated financial statements.
The question concerns accounting in consolidated financial statements under IFRS.

approaches.label: Fair value hedge accounting
approaches.rationale_for_inclusion: IFRS 9 identifies fair value hedge as a top-level hedge accounting model for exposures to changes in fair value of recognised assets or liabilities attributable to a particular risk.

approaches.label: Cash flow hedge accounting
approaches.rationale_for_inclusion: IFRS 9 identifies cash flow hedge as a top-level hedge accounting model for exposure to variability in cash flows attributable to a particular risk, including highly probable forecast transactions.

approaches.label: Net investment hedge accounting
approaches.rationale_for_inclusion: IFRS 9 and IAS 21 identify hedge of a net investment in a foreign operation as a separate top-level hedge accounting model for foreign currency risk.

approaches.label: Ordinary foreign currency accounting
approaches.rationale_for_inclusion: IAS 21 provides the baseline accounting model under which exchange differences on monetary items are recognised in profit or loss unless specific hedge accounting or net investment treatment applies.
